name: BlueBox RO DS
description: The BlueBox RO DS is a portable desalination water filtration system.

taxonomy.sub_category: BlueBox RO DS Updated on January 25, 2024 · Created on September 10, 2020 BlueBox RO DS The BlueBox RO DS is a portable desalination water filtration system. Developed By MuchMoreWater Tested By BlueBox RO DS Content Partners Unknown Info Tags Snapshot Manufacturing & Delivery Performance & Use Research & Standards Feedback Product Description The BlueBox RO DS is a compact desalination water filtration system that comes in several models, including 70 RO and 140 RO . Packaged in a portable travel case, the system utilizes a central reverse osmosis filter and a high-pressure pump to remove salt and microbial life from seawater and brackish water. Target SDGs SDG 6: Clean Water and Sanitation SDG 7: Affordable and Clean Energy Market Suggested Retail Price $18,072.00 Market Suggested Retail Price (Secondary Currency) 15,250 EUR Target Users (Target Impact Group) Household Distributors / Implementing Organizations UNICEF, WHO, DANIDA, Red Cross, FMV, DEMA, DSB Competitive Landscape Direct competitors include Spectra Aquifer 360 . Regions Worldwide Manufacturing/Building Method This product is manufactured and produced in Denmark. Intellectural Property Type Trademark User Provision Model This product is distributed by humanitarian organizations to individual communities in developing countries and disaster zones. Alternatively, the product can be purchased directly from the manufacturer. Distributions to Date Status Unknown Type of system Reverse osmosis filtration Manufacturer-specified water treatment rate (L/hr) 70 RO: 70 L/hr 140 RO: 140 L/hr Bacteria reduction 99.999999% Virus reduction 99.999999% Protozoa reduction 99.999999% Heavy metals and/or arsenic reduction 98.9% reduction for salts and minerals, metals reduction is unknown Power required 1.3 kW electric motor, included Safe water storage capacity (L) None Design Specifications The BlueBox RO DS systems take influent raw seawater or brackish water with a salt concentration of 2500 ppm or greater and pass it through a reverse osmosis filter to remove salt and other minerals as well as microbial life. The system is 900 mm x 600 mm x 400 mm and weighs 57 kg. The operating pressure is 55 bar and the filter purifies at the 5-micron level. The model comes in the 70 and 140 model sizes, each number referring to the flow rate of the system in L/hr. Technical Support Provided by manufacturer. Replacement Components 6-month replacement list: 5-micron filter, pickling solution powder Lifecycle Unknown Manufacturer Specified Performance Parameters This filter was designed to remove 100% of microbes and 98.9% of salts with an assumed influent salt ratio of 2500 ppm or greater. Vetted Performance Status Unknown Safety Unknown Complementary Technical Systems The manufacturer also sells a tools package, active coal cartridge, UV lamp, washer gun, water tester, and education on how to use the system through their own education platform.
